questions.q: In which year were the modern Olympic Games first held?
questions.explanation: The first modern Olympic Games were held in Athens, Greece in 1896, reviving the ancient tradition after nearly 1,600 years. The year 1900 saw the second modern Olympics in Paris, but the inaugural games were in 1896.

questions.q: What does the Olympic symbol with five interlocked rings represent?
questions.explanation: The five rings represent the five continents (Europe, Asia, Africa, Oceania, and the Americas) coming together in the Olympic movement. Each ring's color is found in at least one national flag, symbolizing universal participation.

questions.q: Which ancient Greek city hosted the original Olympic Games?
questions.explanation: The ancient Olympic Games were held in Olympia, a sanctuary dedicated to Zeus in ancient Greece, starting around 776 BCE. The city of Olympia gave its name to these athletic competitions that lasted over 1,000 years.

questions.q: How often are the Olympic Games held?
questions.explanation: The Olympic Games are held every four years, alternating between Summer and Winter Olympics which also occur every four years on different years. This four-year cycle is called an Olympiad.

questions.q: What is the primary purpose of the Olympic torch relay?
options: To warm up athletes for competition
To symbolize the connection between ancient and modern games
To light a promotional fire for the host city
questions.explanation: The Olympic torch relay symbolizes the connection between the ancient Olympic Games and the modern Olympic movement, bringing light and unity to the opening ceremony. The torch is lit in Olympia, Greece and travels to the host city.

questions.q: In which country were the Winter Olympics first held?
questions.explanation: The first Winter Olympics were held in Chamonix, France in 1924. Switzerland hosted the Winter Olympics in 1928 in St. Moritz, making it the second Winter Olympics host.
The official Olympic motto is 'Citius, Altius, Fortius' which is Latin for 'Faster, Higher, Stronger.' This motto was introduced in 1894 and emphasizes the pursuit of excellence in sport.

questions.q: Which sport was NOT part of the ancient Olympic Games?
questions.explanation: Swimming was not part of the ancient Olympic Games, which focused on track and field events, wrestling, and combat sports. The discus, javelin, and wrestling were all ancient Olympic events.

questions.q: How many Olympic rings are there?
questions.explanation: There are exactly five Olympic rings, each representing a continent. The rings are interlocked to symbolize the unity and connection of the world's nations through the Olympic Games.

questions.q: What color is the Olympic flame?
questions.explanation: The Olympic flame is typically orange and yellow due to the burning of fuels used to light it, creating this distinctive color. In rare cases with special fuel mixtures, it may appear different, but the traditional color is orange-yellow.
